assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.
The intragroup dividend receivable is denominated in a foreign currency and creates foreign exchange exposure.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies recognised assets or liabilities as possible hedged items, making fair value hedge a top-level hedge accounting model relevant to a recognised dividend receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies forecast transactions and exposures to variability in cash flows as a distinct hedge accounting model, including intragroup forecast transactions in limited cases.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 treat hedges of foreign currency risk in a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate top-level hedge accounting model in consolidated financial statements.
